author: nflaig
content: <@1386598056320831509> RE https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5035, I am no sure if it's even just simpler do have the node broadcast both current and next epoch, avoids having to implement an extra check in the beacon node to track what it already has broadcast. It would be ignored and not rebroadcast by other nodes due to `[IGNORE] The signed_proposer_preferences is the first valid message`, so I would assume this is fine too

author: bharath.vedartham
content:
I think you would have to check if you broadcasted it anyways right?
Let's say we are at epoch E, we have broadcasted preferences for epoch E and epoch E+1.
Now when we arrive at epoch E+1, we already have broadcasted the preferences for epoch E+1 in epoch E so wouldn't that be duplicate then?

author: nflaig
content: I would leave it up to the implementation, since we wanna use proposer preferences to also populate local caches might be simpler to send both epochs to beacon node each time and let gossip layer handle dedupe
